LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF FARAMARZ GHORBAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1794, PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED $10,550 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES AND $1,331 IN COSTS AND EXPENSES. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES FOR TIME REASONABLY EXPENDED BY MS. YECK. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk KTom; Proceedings not reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-01-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susan Yeck of Lemon Law Advocate telephone number 619-531-7999 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff.
Brian Takahashi of Bowman and Brooke LLP telephone number 310-768-3068 appeared by telephone for Defendant.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FARAMARZ GHORBAN'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs is granted. Pursuant to Civil Code section 1794, Plaintiff is awarded $10,550 in attorney's fees and $1,331 in costs and expenses. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of reasonable attorney fees for time reasonably expended by Ms. Yeck. (Robertson v. Fleetwood (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 785, 818 (citing Nightingale v. Hyundai Motor America,(1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 99,105, fn. 6).) Although block billing is not prohibited by law, courts reduce fee awards for such practices where the time entry description makes it difficult or impossible to assess the reasonableness of the time requested. (Gauchat-Hargis v. Forest River, Inc. (2013) WL 4828594, 6.) There are three entries in Ms. Yeck's block billing records in which Ms. Yeck made a not charged notation. (Declaration Susan Yeck.) The notation makes it difficult to determine the reasonableness of the amount requested because it is not clear how much time was spent on the charged activities. The not charged entries account for $4,100 of the amount of attorney's fees requested. (Declaration Susan Yeck; Entries for 3/10/2015, 4/10/2015, 10/21-11/18/2015.) The amount requested for the instant motion is reduced from $3,400 to $2,200 due to the boilerplate nature of the motion. Thus, a total of $5,300 is subtracted from the total amount of attorney's fees requested. Plaintiff is entitled to costs in the amount of $1,331 because a buyer's recovery of costs in a Song-Beverly-Consumer Warranty Act action is governed by Civil Code section 1794, not California Code of Procedure sections 1032 or 1033.5 (Murillo v. Fleetwood Enterprises, Inc. (1998) 17 Cal.4th 985, 992.) The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
